kind.doc: Player aim control; in normal third-person play the aim is camera-derived.

associated_functions.name: GetAdjustedCameraMatrix
associated_functions.doc:
Returns the matrix used by the aim raycast: the ADS branch reads the alternate-aim transform,
 otherwise the camera transform; `weapon` adds optional ballistic pitch. Static method that
 returns CMatrix4f by value (sret: `result` out-param, also returned in rax).

associated_functions.name: UpdateDirectAim
associated_functions.doc: Raycasts from the camera position along camera-forward to determine the aim target.

associated_functions.name: GetGripPosition
associated_functions.doc: Returns the grip transform for the given hand (E_WEAPON_GRIP_HAND_*, modeled as i32).

kind.doc: Autodesk HumanIK wrapper.

associated_functions.name: AddEffectorTargetPosition
associated_functions.doc:
Pushes an IK effector target (character-local space). Prototype verified against the debug
 PDB. solve_step / pass_info are 4-byte enums (HumanIK::HIKSolveStep / HIKPassInfo), passed
 as i32.

associated_functions.name: UpdateRender
associated_functions.doc:
Per-frame camera update: snapshots the previous view-projection, Lerps the transform between
 T0 and T1 by `dtf`, and recalculates projection and frustum.

associated_functions.name: SetupRenderCamera
associated_functions.doc:
Builds the render-camera matrices once per Draw: applies reverse-Z and TAA jitter to the
 projection, then rebuilds m_ViewProjection / m_ViewProjectionF from m_View. One-shot per
 camera via the m_IsRenderCamera flag (0x20). Returns the last matrix-copy destination.

associated_functions.name: SetComputeView
associated_functions.doc:
Sets (or clears) the m_ComputeView flag bit (0x08). When set, UpdateRender re-derives
 m_View as Inverse(m_TransformF) each frame instead of reading a supplied view matrix.

associated_functions.name: RecalcProjection
associated_functions.doc:
Rebuilds m_Projection from the FOV / near / far / aspect parameters (PerspectiveFov,
 PerspectiveOffCenter or Ortho per the m_UseOffCenter / m_Ortho flags), clears
 m_DirtyProjection, and applies the reverse-Z remap (z' = w - z) only when m_IsRenderCamera
 (0x20) is set.

kind.doc: The per-frame camera pipeline context (opaque).

associated_functions.name: InitTransform
associated_functions.doc:
Sets the active camera's transform, writing both T0 and T1 to `mat` (so the subsequent
 interpolation is constant).

associated_functions.name: InitFOV
associated_functions.doc: Sets the active camera's field of view (both T0 and T1).

associated_functions.name: GetRenderCamera
associated_functions.doc: Returns m_RenderCamera (the camera the render thread reads).

associated_functions.name: UpdateRender
associated_functions.doc:
Sim-path per-frame update: iterates every camera in the manager's list and calls
 Camera::UpdateRender on each. This is where m_ActiveCamera's m_View is produced for the frame.

kind.doc:
Root of the camera pipeline tree: flattens and weights the pipelines and runs their render
 modifiers, populating the render contexts each frame.
A node in the camera pipeline (opaque).

associated_functions.name: PushRenderContext
associated_functions.doc:
Funnels the camera control contexts into the active camera state: reads m_NextRenderContext,
 sets the audio listener, applies the jitter filter, and calls CameraManager InitTransform/InitFOV.

associated_functions.name: UpdateBlackboardValues
associated_functions.doc: Reads the action-effector inputs into m_TransformedGamepadInput.

associated_functions.name: IsInCinematicCamera
associated_functions.doc: True when the currently selected camera in the director stack has the cinematic flag set.

associated_functions.name: IsAlternateAimTransformUsed
associated_functions.doc: True when the alternate-aim (ADS) transform is in use.

associated_functions.name: UpdateRender
associated_functions.doc:
Per-frame render update: runs the camera tree (UpdateRenderContexts), pushes the render
 context, then updates the lighting and water level at the camera.

kind.doc: The camera arm/boom; the look delta rotates it.

associated_functions.name: DeltaTransform
associated_functions.doc: Apply a (yaw, pitch, roll) delta-angle to the boom.

associated_functions.name: CalculateInputDeltaAngles
associated_functions.doc:
Read the look effectors, combine them, apply per-axis sensitivity, and return the
 delta-angle (yaw, pitch).

associated_functions.name: ProcessCameraContext
associated_functions.doc:
Caller of CalculateInputDeltaAngles: takes the returned delta-angle and applies it to the
 camera via BoomTransform::DeltaTransform (the call is at +0x17D).

associated_functions.name: UpdatePropEffects
associated_functions.doc: Per-frame update of the character's attached prop visual effects. `dt` is the frame delta.

associated_functions.name: Update
associated_functions.doc:
Per-frame tick: measures the QueryPerformanceCounter delta and updates m_SPF (an exponential
 moving average), m_RealSPF, m_FPS, and m_GameTimeMicro.

fields.name: m_Weather
fields.doc: The live Weather (also returned by GetWeather).

associated_functions.name: GetWeather
associated_functions.doc: Returns m_Weather.

associated_functions.name: Apply
associated_functions.doc:
In-engine reference that reads and writes both the time-of-day and the weather each cycle --
 the canonical example of driving both systems.

fields.name: m_Atmosphere
fields.doc: Reach the weather via Atmosphere::GetWeather / m_Weather.

fields.name: m_SnowRatio
fields.doc: 0..1 snow blend. From the dump's struct layout; not instruction-verified.

fields.name: m_RainIntensity
fields.doc: 0..1 rain intensity. From the dump's struct layout; not instruction-verified.

fields.name: m_Severity
fields.doc: Storm severity, ~0.1 clear .. ~4.0 full storm.

associated_functions.name: SetSeverity
associated_functions.doc:
Set m_Severity (~0.1 clear .. ~4.0 storm) and clear m_DoUpdateSeverity. NOTE:
 WeatherController's update overwrites m_Severity toward its own target unless a force flag
 or a named weather event holds it -- fire an event (see WeatherController) to pin a state.

associated_functions.name: UpdateSeverityTarget
associated_functions.doc: Smoothly drive severity toward `target` over `update_time` seconds.

associated_functions.name: Init
associated_functions.doc:
Subscribes the named weather events: weather_sunny / weather_rain / weather_snow /
 weather_restore / weather_instant, plus cloud_base / cloud_height. Firing one (via the
 engine's event send) is the robust way to hold a weather state, since the controller's
 per-frame update otherwise overwrites the Weather scalars. Event severities: rain ->
 severity 4.0 / intensity 1.0 / snow 0; snow -> severity 4.0 / snow 1.0; sunny -> severity 0.1.

fields.name: m_CurrentTimeOfDay
fields.doc:
Current time of day in hours (0-24). The render engine copies this out each frame, so a
 write here propagates to lighting.

associated_functions.name: SetTimeOfDay
associated_functions.doc:
Set the time of day in hours (0-24): fmods to 24, fires the per-hour event, clamps against
 m_PauseTimeOfDay. Preferred over writing m_CurrentTimeOfDay directly.

associated_functions.name: Update
associated_functions.doc: Top-level per-frame update (advances the sim and drives rendering). Returns false on exit.

associated_functions.name: UpdateRender
associated_functions.doc: Builds the per-frame update contexts and drives rendering.

kind.doc:
Primitive topology passed to the draw wrappers (Graphics::EPrimitiveType). Patchlists 0x21-0x24
 are tessellation control-point counts.
CGraphicsEngine::DrawThreadTaskParam

One reflection-proxy depth-history slot (the planar / water reflection state machine). Five of
 these live on the graphics engine; the lifecycle byte advances once per scene dispatch.

fields.name: m_DepthTexture
fields.doc: Reflection-proxy depth texture (Graphics::HTexture_t handle).

fields.name: m_FrameIndex
fields.doc: Lifecycle counter: 0 = free, 2 -> promote to 3, 3 = pick, else += 1.

fields.name: m_GBufferTexture
fields.doc: Deferred-shading render targets ("GBuffer0".."GBuffer3").

fields.name: m_VelocityBufferTexture
fields.doc: Motion-blur velocity buffer.

fields.name: m_MainDepthTexture
fields.doc: Main scene depth ("MainDepthBuffer").

fields.name: m_MainColorBuffer
fields.doc: Main scene HDR color ("MainColorBuffer").

fields.name: m_DownSampledDepthTexture
fields.doc: Hi-Z / downsampled depth ("DownsampledDepth").

fields.name: m_EffectInfo
fields.doc: Reflection-proxy depth-history (planar / water reflection state machine), 5 slots.

fields.name: m_BackBufferLinear
fields.doc: Final linear back buffer ("BackBufferLinear").

fields.name: m_EffectInfoIndex
fields.doc: Index of the reflection-proxy slot picked this frame.

associated_functions.name: Draw
associated_functions.doc:
Graphics entry point: runs the per-frame prologue (presents the previous frame, advances the
 clock and constant-buffer pools) then dispatches this frame's draw.

associated_functions.name: Flip
associated_functions.doc: Presents the previous frame's back buffer.

associated_functions.name: DispatchDraw
associated_functions.doc: Queues this frame's render work on the render thread.

associated_functions.name: HandleDrawThreadTask
associated_functions.doc:
Render-thread body: gbuffer, lighting, post-effects and UI. `param` is
 DrawThreadTaskParam* (layout TBD).

associated_functions.name: TextureCachePlatformUpdate
associated_functions.doc:
Draw-prologue step. Copies m_ActiveCamera into the engine-owned render-camera slot
 (this + 0x170), runs CCamera::SetupRenderCamera on it, publishes it as CameraManager's
 m_RenderCamera, then runs the per-frame texture-cache update under the context lock. `ctx`
 is Graphics::HContext_t* (opaque).

kind.doc: Graphics::HContext_t (GPU context handle)
Graphics::HDevice_t (GPU device handle)
Graphics::HRenderSetup_t (a render-target configuration a pass draws into).
Graphics::HTexture_t (GPU texture handle).

The per-view render context the render passes read: camera matrices (view, projection, the
 translation-free offset view-projection and the separate camera world position), shadow data and
 per-frame flags. Filled each dispatch by RenderPass::SetRenderContextCamera.
Anti-aliasing resolve mode (AntiAliasingEffect::m_Mode).

Anti-aliasing resolve. `m_Mode`: 1/4 = FXAA, 2 = SMAA 1x, 3 = SMAA T2X. Mode 3 adds a temporal
 reprojection against a previous-frame history texture.

associated_functions.name: Apply
associated_functions.doc: `slot` is the in/out post-effect result-slot index.

associated_functions.name: ApplySubsampleJitter
associated_functions.doc:
Post-multiplies the sub-pixel clip-space jitter translation onto `proj`, but only when
 m_Mode == 3 (SMAA T2X); the phase comes from the previous-frame counter parity.

associated_functions.name: CreateRenderTargetResources
associated_functions.doc:
Allocates the AA's render-target resources (the T2X history ping-pong textures and their
 render setups) sized `width` x `height`.

kind.doc: Gaussian blur (the non-bokeh blur path).
Bokeh blur (the IsBokehActive path; runs after DownScale2x2PackFocus).
Bokeh depth-of-field downscale prepass (2x2 pack + focus).
Alpha-blended fade quad over the scene.
Bloom / glare generator (writes its own scratch targets, composited later).
Red damage vignette. Slot-passthrough (returns the input slot index).

fields.name: m_AutoExposureKey
fields.doc:
The auto-exposure target numerator ("key"): ToneMappingEffect::Update sets the exposure target
 to this divided by the raw-brightness histogram mid-point.

kind.doc: Per-frame render-context flags.

associated_functions.name: ApplyWorldFilters
associated_functions.doc:
Wires up and enqueues the world post-effect block, then runs the world fade accumulator
 (ApplyWorldFadeFilter). `dt` flows only into that accumulator; the texture arguments are
 the scene inputs.

associated_functions.name: ApplyGlobalFilters
associated_functions.doc:
Enqueues the global post-effect block and advances its dt-driven accumulators: the screen
 fade alpha (clamped [0,1]) and the sun-direction / heat-haze accumulator.

associated_functions.name: ApplyWorldFadeFilter
associated_functions.doc: The world fade accumulator stepped by ApplyWorldFilters' dt.

associated_functions.name: IsBokehActive
associated_functions.doc:
True when the bokeh depth-of-field path is active (selects the downscale + bokeh blur over
 the plain blur).

associated_functions.name: IsMotionBlurActive
associated_functions.doc: True when motion blur is active.

associated_functions.name: ApplySubsampleJitter
associated_functions.doc: Post-multiplies the AA's sub-pixel TAA jitter onto `proj` (effective only at AA mode 3).

kind.doc:
The render block for the RP_POSTEFFECTS pass. Its Draw runs the HDR post chain in order:
 histogram generation, sun-halo pre-apply, blur (bokeh or plain), glare, depth of field, motion
 blur, the HDR->LDR tonemap (DrawHistogramWindow), player-damage vignette, anti-aliasing, sun
 halo + additive blend, and the final fade. It threads a single result-texture slot index through
 the slot-returning effects (DoF / motion blur / damage / AA), hopping between the three
 fullscreen temp textures. `ctx` is RenderContext*; `info` is a CRBIInfo*.
Sun halo. PreApply prepares and sets the ready flag (byte at +0x114); Apply composites it.

associated_functions.name: PostDraw
associated_functions.doc: Late render-pass step (finalizes / copies render targets under the context mutex).

associated_functions.name: DrawRenderPassRange
associated_functions.doc:
Draws every render block in the half-open pass-index range [first, last): for each pass it
 walks the fixed RenderPass* array at this + 32*pass + 128 and vtable-dispatches each block.
 GBuffer is 0x2F..0x55, the lighting/scene block 0x56..0x96, post-effects 0x96..0x97.

associated_functions.name: DrawGBuffer
associated_functions.doc:
GBuffer fill: binds two global textures, then DrawRenderPassRange(0x2F, 0x55) (depth /
 velocity prefix, static/dynamic models, decals).

associated_functions.name: Draw
associated_functions.doc:
Lighting / reflections / opaque / environment / water / transparency:
 DrawRenderPassRange(0x56, 0x96), then clears the global texture samplers.

associated_functions.name: DrawPosteffects
associated_functions.doc:
Post-effects pass: DrawRenderPassRange(0x96, 0x97) (the RP_POSTEFFECTS pass, whose block is
 RenderBlockPostEffects::Draw).

associated_functions.name: SetGlobalShaderConstants
associated_functions.doc:
Uploads the global per-view constant buffer for the frame: lighting, fog, wetness, and the
 render camera's full (translation-bearing) m_ViewProjectionF and world position. This block
 drives screen-space / non-geometry work, not opaque-geometry vertex placement.

associated_functions.name: ApplyJitterTransform
associated_functions.doc:
Per-frame TAA jitter: forwards to PostEffectsManager::ApplySubsampleJitter, which
 post-multiplies a sub-pixel clip-space translation onto `proj` only when AA mode == 3.

associated_functions.name: EraseAllDeletedRenderBlocks
associated_functions.doc:
Drains a separate deferred deletion list of render blocks (under its own critical section).
 Does not touch the per-pass draw lists.

kind.doc:
The flat, contiguous render-pass id enum (ERenderPass). Every `pass` / `first` / `last` index in
 the render engine is one of these. The pass-index ranges the render engine draws by:
 GBuffer = RP_Z_OCCLUDERS..RP_LAST_GBUFFER, scene = RP_REFLECTIVE_WATER_PLANES..RP_LAST_MAIN,
 post-effects = RP_POSTEFFECTS. Named `RenderPassId` to avoid clashing with the `RenderPass` type
 (the CRenderPass class in render_pass.pyxis).
The per-frame constant-buffer ring.

associated_functions.name: HandBackBuffers
associated_functions.doc: Recycles last frame's constant buffers from the in-use stack back to the free pool.

kind.doc:
One of a render pass's double-buffered render-block-item lists. Add appends entries
 (InterlockedExchangeAdd on m_NumElements); on overflow (m_NumElements >= m_ListSize) it spills
 into the global overflow list. DoDraw reads min(m_ListSize, m_NumElements) entries and never
 writes m_NumElements, so a populated list can be redrawn any number of times.

fields.name: m_List
fields.doc: Array of 0x20-byte entries.

fields.name: m_ListSize
fields.doc: Capacity.

fields.name: m_NumElements
fields.doc: Live element count (volatile).

associated_functions.name: Add
associated_functions.doc: Appends one render-block-item; spills to the global overflow list on capacity overflow.

associated_functions.name: SetupRenderFrameData
associated_functions.doc:
Appends `count` render-block-items (`items`) to the active RBILists draw/add lists (`a3`
 holds the lists). Static (no `this`). Called per batch, including from CPU fragment worker
 threads -- not once per frame. The argument list matters: it dereferences `a3 + 0x8038`.

associated_functions.name: SetRenderContextCamera
associated_functions.doc:
Fills the per-view RenderContext from `camera` (the render camera, or a shadow/reflective
 light camera selected by the pass flags): view, projection and offset (translation-free)
 view-projection via CalculateOffsetViewProjectionMatrix, the separate camera world position,
 and the parity-buffered shadow matrices ((render_frame_counters.m_FrameIndex & 1) stride).
 Static (no `this`).

associated_functions.name: SaveRenderFrameData
associated_functions.doc:
The per-pass half of the list rotation (vtable slot 3, driven by RotateRenderFrameData):
 points m_CurrentAddList at m_Lists[parity], m_CurrentDrawList at the other, and zeroes the
 new add-list's element count. Also snapshots the pass camera.

associated_functions.name: DoDraw
associated_functions.doc:
Draws m_CurrentDrawList: walks min(m_ListSize, m_NumElements) blocks via a local cursor and
 vtable-dispatches each. Non-destructive -- never writes m_NumElements (vtable slot 2).

fields.name: m_Format
fields.doc: DXGI_FORMAT (e.g. 28 = R8G8B8A8_UNORM).

kind.doc:
Per-frame brightness histogram used for eye adaptation. Embedded in ToneMappingEffect at +8; its
 occlusion-query bucket counts are what the exposure read-back consumes.

fields.name: m_NumPixelsInBuckets
fields.doc:
Per-bucket pixel counts: one occlusion query per bucket counting samples whose luminance is
 at or above the bucket's threshold. Only the first m_NumBuckets entries are live.

fields.name: m_HistogramBrightPoint
fields.doc: Bright-point computed from the buckets; drives the exposure target.

fields.name: m_HistogramMidPoint
fields.doc: Mid-point computed from the buckets.

kind.doc: N-frame exposure smoother (a ring-buffer average; advances once per render, with no dt term).
HDR tone-mapping / auto-exposure (eye adaptation).

fields.name: m_Histogram
fields.doc: The exposure histogram: CalculateMidAndBrightPointForHistogram fills it, Update reads it.

fields.name: m_HistogramPingPong
fields.doc: Ping-pong selector for the exposure-weighted histogram metering, flipped each frame by Update.

fields.name: m_Histogram2
fields.doc:
A second histogram, processed by Update's second CalculateMidAndBrightPointForHistogram call.
 Its m_HistogramMidPoint is the divisor of the auto-exposure target (target = key / midpoint),
 so this -- not m_Histogram -- is what the converged m_CurrentExposure actually tracks.

fields.name: m_NumBuckets
fields.doc: The active histogram bucket count.

fields.name: m_CurrentExposure
fields.doc:
The current clamped/smoothed auto-exposure multiplier. Written by Update once per frame and
 read back into the next frame's GenerateHistogramForFinalScene metering (the feedback loop).

associated_functions.name: GenerateHistogramForFinalScene
associated_functions.doc:
Builds the auto-exposure histogram for the final scene and writes the current histogram slot
 indices to a6 / a7 (out-params); returns a7. Meters luminance through a
 "LuminanceToDepthWithExposure" shader fed the previous frame's m_CurrentExposure as a
 constant, so metering is exposure-weighted -- the histogram feeds back into the exposure that
 weights the next frame's metering (via CalculateMidAndBrightPointForHistogram and Update).

associated_functions.name: DrawHistogramWindow
associated_functions.doc:
Meters the second, NON-exposure-weighted scene-luminance histogram (m_Histogram2) -- it calls
 PopulateHistogram with a fixed exposure of 1.0, so it measures raw scene brightness, which is
 the value Update divides the auto-exposure target by. Runs once per dispatch in the post chain,
 like GenerateHistogramForFinalScene (which meters the exposure-weighted m_Histogram).

associated_functions.name: Update
associated_functions.doc:
The per-frame eye-adaptation step: runs CalculateMidAndBrightPointForHistogram over both
 histograms, then writes the new m_CurrentExposure (target = ctx.m_AutoExposureKey /
 m_Histogram2 midpoint, clamped, then adapted). Runs once per real frame from
 CPostEffectsManager::UpdateRender.

kind.doc:
Action ID, indexing the static action-name-to-ID table (action_name_table at 0x142_D99_370).
 255 actions (PAUSE..GUI_USE_BUTTON); the numbering is fixed at build time, so IDs can be
 hardcoded. The engine takes a raw int for these, and this enum is repr-int, so it transmutes
 cleanly into the action_id / action parameters.
Digital state of an InputDeviceEffector (the m_State enum). IsSet/IsPressed are state in {2, 3}.
Maps action IDs to effector slots (255 action IDs total).

One input effector slot. Layout from the debug PDB (Input::InputDeviceEffector, 0x14),
 cross-checked against retail usage (m_Value@0, m_State@8, m_StateTime@0x10 all match). The
 pointer GetActionEffector returns is the head of a linked-list node whose extra id/next fields
 follow this struct; reading the effector itself uses these offsets.

associated_functions.name: Click
associated_functions.doc: Sets m_Value to 1.0 and m_State to Clicked (a one-frame press edge).

associated_functions.name: Press
associated_functions.doc: Sets m_Value and m_State to Pressed/Held.

associated_functions.name: Freeze
associated_functions.doc: Latches the effector into the Frozen state (ignores the device poll until cleared).

associated_functions.name: ForceClick
associated_functions.doc: Sets m_ForceClick so the click survives the next per-frame poll (UpdateForceClicks consumes it).

kind.doc:
The local player's action map: a write-side wrapper that drives effectors by action ID. The
 global at 0x142_F38_740 holds the instance pointer. Invalid action IDs resolve to a shared
 null-effector sentinel, which the setters guard against. Action-name-to-ID is a static table
 (action_name_table), so action IDs are stable across builds and can be referenced directly.

associated_functions.name: ForceSetPressed
associated_functions.doc: Drives an analog / held action: sets the effector's value (and presses it).

associated_functions.name: ForceSetClicked
associated_functions.doc: Drives a one-frame click for an action (a press edge that survives the poll).

associated_functions.name: Update
associated_functions.doc:
Per-frame device poll: reads all devices and refreshes their effector states. Runs
 UpdateForceClicks afterwards. Effector writes injected before this are overwritten by the
 poll; inject after it instead.

associated_functions.name: UpdateForceClicks
associated_functions.doc: Applies the m_ForceClick flags latched on effectors so an injected click survives the poll.

associated_functions.name: SetupTargetDir
associated_functions.doc:
Read the move effectors (action IDs 28-31) via the action-map GetEffector, rotate the raw
 vector camera-relative, and write the raw and camera-relative move directions to the
 character blackboard.

kind.doc:
Opaque per-task state-machine context passed to character/task Update functions
 (SStateContext). Layout TBD; used behind pointers.

`CMatrix4f` -- the engine's 4x4 matrix. D3D convention: row-major storage, row vectors (a point
 is a row vector left-multiplied by the matrix: `clip = p * M`). The 16 floats are 4 rows; a
 world/camera transform's rows are its basis vectors: `data[0..2]` = right (+X), `data[4..6]` =
 up (+Y), `data[8..10]` = +Z basis (so forward = `-data[8..10]`), `data[12..14]` = translation.
 Right-handed, Y-up. The glam conversions below bridge to glam's column-vector convention by
 transposing (row-major rows become glam columns), so glam matrix math on a converted `Matrix4`
 works without an explicit transpose; to build an engine transform in glam, set the basis
 vectors as the glam COLUMNS (right, up, -forward, translation) and use `to_cols_array`.

associated_functions.name: Multiply4x4
associated_functions.doc:
`CMatrix4f::Multiply4x4` -- the engine's 4x4 product, `result = a * b` (row-major: result
 row i = a's row i times b). E.g. `Multiply4x4(View, Proj, ViewProjection)` gives
 `VP = View * Proj` and `clip = p * View * Proj`. Static (no `this`).

kind.doc:
Scaleform render-target binding. The UI's m_RenderBuffer points at one of these; UpdateData
 swaps the views it holds (RTV @ +0x20, DSV @ +0x28, depth buffer @ +0x08), refcounting them.
 Pointing it at an offscreen RTV redirects where the UI HAL renders. Not tied to startup, so the
 rebind can happen at any time.

associated_functions.name: UpdateData
associated_functions.doc:
Binds the views into this RenderTargetData (RTV @ +0x20, DSV @ +0x28, depth @ +0x08),
 releasing the old ones and AddRef-ing the new. `self` is the inner RenderTargetData at
 `*(render_buffer + 0x20)`.

kind.doc:
Screen-position code returned by the UI world-to-screen / marker placement: 0 = on-screen, 1-8 =
 off-screen clamped edge/corner, 9 = off-screen in front (no clamp), 10 = off-screen behind.

Scaleform-backed UI manager (the concrete class behind IUIManager; a single instance). It renders
 the HUD into the engine surface; InitPlatformRT rebinds its render target.

fields.name: m_RenderBuffer
fields.doc: The Scaleform RenderBuffer the UI HAL renders into (set up by InitPlatformRT).

fields.name: m_ViewWidth
fields.doc: Viewport width / height the world-to-screen mapping (Convert3DCoords) maps NDC into.

associated_functions.name: InitPlatformRT
associated_functions.doc:
Bind the UI render target: build a Scaleform RenderTargetData (m_RenderBuffer) from the
 engine surface's RTV/DSV (GetRTVFromSurface / GetDSVFromSurface) via RenderTargetData::UpdateData.
 Called at startup (InitializeSystem) and on every device/resolution reset (RestoreAfterReset);
 `a2` carries the target dimensions.

associated_functions.name: RestoreAfterReset
associated_functions.doc:
Re-runs InitPlatformRT after a device/resolution reset (`a2` is the new width; the dimensions
 otherwise track the engine surface).

associated_functions.name: Convert3DCoords
associated_functions.doc:
World-to-screen: project `world` through `vp`, divide by w, aspect-correct, and map NDC to
 pixels (m_ViewWidth/Height). Returns false when the point is behind the camera.

associated_functions.name: Get2DInfo
associated_functions.doc:
Marker placement: Convert3DCoords with the supplied `vp`, plus an on-screen test and an
 off-screen edge-clamp (ClampToScreen). Gameplay markers route through here.

associated_functions.name: ClampToScreen
associated_functions.doc:
Clamp an off-screen point to the screen-rect edge/corner (atan2 against the corners) and set
 the position code. Static.

associated_functions.name: StartRender
associated_functions.doc: Begin the UI render job (post to the async render thread).

associated_functions.name: SyncRender
associated_functions.doc: Barrier: spin-waits until the async UI render job has finished.

associated_functions.name: Submit
associated_functions.doc: Final commit: submit the render HAL's output under a graphics scoped lock.

associated_functions.name: RenderStaticBackGround
associated_functions.doc: Draws the pause / menu static background (vtable method).

associated_functions.name: RenderOffScreenTextures
associated_functions.doc: Renders Scaleform UI to offscreen textures for in-world screens (not the HUD; vtable method).

associated_functions.name: IsUsingStaticBackGround
associated_functions.doc: True when a static background is being shown.

functions.name: NAutoAimToTarget_Update
functions.doc: Character-state task that overrides the aim direction toward a locked target. Free function.

functions.name: NHandIKTask_Update
functions.doc: Per-frame hand-IK driver (sources its targets from weapon grip positions). Free function.

functions.name: SetListenerTransform
functions.doc: Sets the 3D audio listener pose and velocity. Free function.

functions.name: GetMasterContext
functions.doc:
Returns the master context (the wrapper around the D3D11 immediate context). `device` is
 Graphics::HDevice_t* (opaque, unused).

functions.name: DrawIndexed
functions.doc: Indexed draw (`Graphics::DrawIndexed`).

functions.name: Draw
functions.doc: Non-indexed draw (`Graphics::Draw`).

functions.name: DrawInstanced
functions.doc: Instanced draw (`Graphics::DrawInstanced`).

functions.name: DrawIndexedInstanced
functions.doc: Indexed instanced draw (`Graphics::DrawIndexedInstanced`).

functions.name: DrawInstancedIndirect
functions.doc: GPU-driven instanced draw (`Graphics::DrawInstancedIndirect`).

functions.name: DrawIndexedInstancedIndirect
functions.doc: GPU-driven indexed instanced draw (`Graphics::DrawIndexedInstancedIndirect`).

functions.name: Dispatch
functions.doc: Compute dispatch (`Graphics::Dispatch`).

functions.name: DispatchIndirect
functions.doc: GPU-driven compute dispatch (`Graphics::DispatchIndirect`).

functions.name: SetRenderSetup
functions.doc: Bind a render setup -- the render-target configuration a pass draws into.

functions.name: Clear
functions.doc: Clear the currently-bound render setup (`color` is a 4-float RGBA pointer, may be null).

functions.name: CopySurfaceToTexture
functions.doc: Copy one surface into another texture.

functions.name: ResolveSurface
functions.doc: Resolve an MSAA surface.

functions.name: GetRTVFromSurface
functions.doc: Returns the surface's render-target view (`*(surface + 0x18)`).

functions.name: GetDSVFromSurface
functions.doc: Returns the surface's depth-stencil view (`*(surface + 0x20)`).

functions.name: graphics_flip
functions.doc:
Low-level present (Graphics::Flip); returns Graphics::EResult. `device` is
 Graphics::HDevice_t* (opaque).

functions.name: CalculateOffsetViewProjectionMatrix
functions.doc:
Copies `src` (a view matrix), zeroes its translation row ({0,0,0,1}), and multiplies by `proj`,
 writing the translation-free OffsetViewProjection into `dst`. This is the view-projection opaque
 (camera-relative) geometry actually uses; the camera world position is supplied separately.
 Static (no `this`).

functions.name: RotateRenderFrameData
functions.doc:
The per-frame render-block-item list rotation, run once in each `CGraphicsEngine::Draw`
 (0x1400F4170) prologue (its call site at 0x1400F4340 is mislabeled `CKeep1000Frames` in this
 binary's symbols). Toggles the global add/draw parity at `0x142ED7680`, then for every render
 pass swaps `m_CurrentAddList`/`m_CurrentDrawList` to the new parity (via `RenderPass`'s vtable
 slot 3) and zeroes the new add-list's element count, and finally flushes the render-block-item
 overflow list. Static (no `this`); reads the render engine + parity from globals. This -- not the
 per-batch `SetupRenderFrameData` build above -- is the actual draw-list swap.

functions.name: CalculateMidAndBrightPointForHistogram
functions.doc:
Computes the histogram mid / bright points (the per-frame eye-adaptation lerp). Free function;
 `hist` is the target SHistogramGeneration.

functions.name: GetIUIManager
functions.doc: Returns the single UIManager instance (the concrete class behind IUIManager).

functions.name: WndProc
functions.doc: The game's Win32 window procedure: LRESULT WndProc(HWND, UINT msg, WPARAM, LPARAM).
